name: file_patch
description: Replace an exact substring in a file, atomically and under an advisory exclusive lock. Safe for concurrent agents: if another agent is patching the same file, this tool waits (up to lock_timeout_ms) then either succeeds or returns a retryable error. The patch is rejected if old_text appears more than once — add more surrounding context to make it unique. If the file is not found, returns the closest match so the agent can self-correct. On success, returns patch context (before/match/after) and size metadata.

path.description: Path to the file to patch. Any format accepted (absolute, relative, ~, $HOME, %USERPROFILE%, Windows, UNC). If not found, error includes a suggestion.

old_text.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must match byte-for-byte including whitespace and line endings (\n vs \r\n). Must appear exactly once in the file — if it appears more than once, add surrounding lines for context. If not found, the error includes a file_preview to help diagnose the mismatch.

new_text.description: The replacement text. May be empty (to delete old_text). May be longer or shorter than old_text.

lock_timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait for the exclusive file lock when another agent is patching the same file. Default 5000 (5 seconds). Set to 0 for a single non-blocking attempt.
